env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with a running RTVI-CV container reachable at `http://localhost:9000/api/v1` (start it with the DEPLOY flow before running these checks — see `eval/deploy-evals.json`). The container must already have at least the readiness, liveness, startup, metrics, and stream-management endpoints reachable. `docker`, `curl`, and `jq` available on the host. The `/stream/add` test below uses the DeepStream sample mp4 at `/opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4`, which is baked into the RTVI-CV (DeepStream-based) image — no host-side staging is required. This eval exercises the API USAGE flow of the `vss-deploy-detection-tracking-2d` skill against the live REST endpoint — it must not invoke `/deploy`, redeploy the container, or call any VSS profile.

expects.query: Add a stream file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4 with id cam_entrance to rtvi-cv.
checks: The agent calls `POST http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/add` against the live RTVI-CV instance.
The request body contains `camera_url` exactly equal to `file:///opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/samples/streams/sample_1080p_h264.mp4` and a `camera_id` of `cam_entrance`.
The agent renders the curl command in a confirmation box BEFORE executing the POST (per the skill's universal API-box rule).
The agent renders the API response as a formatted table or structured block.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Is rtvi-cv ready? Check the health.
checks: The agent calls all three readiness endpoints: `GET http://localhost:9000/api/v1/live`, `/api/v1/ready`, and `/api/v1/startup`.
The agent uses GET requests (read-only) and does not require per-call user confirmation.
The final response is a combined summary of the live, ready, and startup states (e.g. all three returned 200, or which one failed).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: What is the FPS on all streams? Get rtvi-cv metrics.
checks: The agent calls `GET http://localhost:9000/api/v1/metrics`.
The final response presents per-stream FPS plus at least one of {GPU utilization, GPU memory, CPU usage} in a formatted table or structured block.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: List all active streams in rtvi-cv.
checks: The agent calls `GET http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/get-stream-info`.
The final response presents active streams as a formatted table with at least camera id and stream URL columns (status when available).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
The agent FIRST calls `GET http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/get-stream-info` to discover the currently active streams before issuing any removal.

The agent presents the live stream list to the user (e.g. via an AskQuestion / structured prompt) so they can pick which `camera_id` to remove — it does NOT pick or fabricate a `camera_id` on its own.
If the live stream list is empty, the agent reports that no streams are active and exits WITHOUT calling `/stream/remove`.

The agent only calls `POST http://localhost:9000/api/v1/stream/remove` against a `camera_id` that appeared in the `stream-list` response from the prior GET — it does NOT remove any stream id that was not in the live list.
The `/stream/remove` request body contains both `camera_id` and a `camera_url` taken verbatim from the matching entry in the prior `stream-list` response.
The agent renders the `/stream/remove` curl command in a confirmation box BEFORE executing the POST (per the skill's universal API-box rule for destructive calls).
The agent renders the API response as a formatted table or structured block.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
